Tests if parsing a regular expression with a paragraph separator returns NaN.

# Purpose
This code is a test case that verifies the behavior of the `parseInt` function when it is given a `RegExp` object containing the Unicode character `\u2029` (paragraph separator). The `assert` function checks that the result of `parseInt` is `NaN`, which confirms that `parseInt` does not parse the input as a number. This test ensures that the `parseInt` function correctly handles non-numeric input by returning `NaN`, as expected according to the ECMAScript specification. The code is part of a test suite to validate JavaScript's built-in functions.
